name: the Pacific
summary: The ocean on whose islands, according to what the deathless Chinamen told old Castro, remains of the Great Old Ones were still to be found as Cyclopean stones.
description:
The Pacific enters the cult's lore through Castro's confession: remains of the Things that once ruled the earth, the deathless Chinamen had told him, were still to be found as Cyclopean stones on islands in the Pacific.

The claim was part of what Castro said he had learned from the undying leaders of the cult, and it points the whole legend seaward — toward the stone survivals of the Great Old Ones.

sources.quote: Remains of Them, he said the deathless Chinamen had told him, were still to be found as Cyclopean stones on islands in the Pacific.
sources.context: Chapter 2, The Tale of Inspector Legrasse
